evidence_quotes.quote: The situation of PC games needs many improvements, but I personally consider the idea of keeping access to them in the best form to be the most essential.

The destruction of these worlds merely in order to cut costs or to push consumers to the next product should be rejected and regulated as art, not as a stripped product from which you discover when profit no longer originates.
I believe that at least access, in the most basic sense, should be facilitated for those who spend their hard money on these games

paragraphs.text: The situation of PC games needs many improvements, but I personally consider the idea of keeping access to them in the best form to be the most essential. The current system is an affront to the effort and work of so many creative minds who have made a collective effort to create a world that the general public can explore. Personally, I have so many fantastic memories created by the universe, characters, and even the music that I experienced in this environment. The destruction of these worlds merely in order to cut costs or to push consumers to the next product should be rejected and regulated as art, not as a stripped product from which you discover when profit no longer originates. I believe that at least access, in the most basic sense, should be facilitated for those who spend their hard money on these games
